message:
fix(core,time): add generational handle safety to cleanup stack and timer wheel

The cleanup stack previously used raw slot indices as handles, which
meant that a handle obtained before a drain-and-reuse cycle could
silently address the wrong cleanup entry.  This is a use-after-free
class bug in safety-critical teardown paths.

Changes to the cleanup subsystem (cleanup.h, cleanup.c):
- Add a per-slot uint16_t generation counter to asx_cleanup_stack.
- Encode generation into the upper 16 bits of asx_cleanup_handle via
  cleanup_make_handle() / cleanup_handle_slot() / cleanup_handle_generation().
- Bump generation on every push (skipping zero to distinguish from
  uninitialised state).
- Validate generation on pop — stale handles now return ASX_E_NOT_FOUND
  instead of silently cancelling the wrong entry.
- Shrink stack->count on LIFO pop sequences so that repeated
  push/pop cycles do not falsely exhaust capacity.

Changes to the timer wheel (timer_wheel.c):
- Widen timer slot generation from uint16_t to uint32_t for longer
  epoch safety in high-churn HFT workloads.
- Extract asx_timer_next_generation() helper that skips zero, matching
  cleanup stack convention.
- asx_timer_wheel_reset() now bumps per-slot generations instead of
  zeroing them, so handles from a previous epoch are correctly rejected
  after wheel reset without requiring a full reinit.

These changes are safety-critical for any runtime path that caches
cleanup or timer handles across lifecycle boundaries (drain/reset).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
